title: My Safe Florida Home program homepage
note: Current public workflow and report-stage framing.

title: Program FAQ
note: Inspection and grant remain separate and contractor liability stays with the homeowner.

title: MSFH Support Center hub
note: Operational source of truth for post-report confusion states.

title: Grant eligibility
note: Confirms recommended improvements, 24-month timing, and denial risk for work started before approval.

title: Initial inspection report timing and contents
note: Report includes recommended improvements, cost estimates, and the 1802 summary.

title: Recommended improvements article
note: Explains where to find recommended improvements and why opening-protection scope needs careful reading.

title: Review and select eligible mitigation projects
note: Attached homes treated as townhouses can be limited to opening-protection-only funding.

title: Authorized mitigation improvements
note: Authorized improvements remain opening protection, roof-to-wall, roof deck attachment, and SWR.

title: Choosing a licensed contractor
note: The homeowner must choose and manage the contractor.

title: Working with a contractor flyer
note: The old authorized contractor list ended and homeowners should compare quotes and documents themselves.

title: Can I replace my roof?
note: Roof replacement may align only when required to complete recommended eligible work.

title: Building permits responsibility
note: Permits still need to be obtained and closed before final inspection.

title: No recommended improvements
note: Without recommended improvements the grant path cannot proceed.

title: 2025 Florida Senate update
note: Shows recent program scale and funding movement.

title: Consumer alert on imposters
note: Confirms scam risk and why non-affiliation language must stay visible.
